raw_output:
PR #7 security review (Codex CLI 0.145.0, gpt-5.6-sol, read-only sandbox).

Verbatim: "Z1 (low): precision = 2 strengthens enforcement but does not make fail_under = 99 exact. coverage.py evaluates round(total, 2) < 99, so totals from approximately 98.995% through 98.999...% still pass. The ADR and plan overstate this as fully 'closed' and as making any breach fail; the blind spot is reduced from roughly 0.5 points to 0.005 points, not eliminated. Overall assessment: The change is monotonic and does not weaken existing coverage enforcement. It adds no dependency, code-execution path, privilege, or consumer-agent automation risk. Its only security-relevant side effect is stricter coverage gating and two-decimal report formatting; the remaining issue is documentation overstating the exactness of the 99% floor."

--- Post-review delta note (orchestrator, attested at final head 57bb1f2) ---
Single-round battery on 1c-file diff converged with zero blocking findings.
Post-review commit 57bb1f2 applies the validated wording fixes: ADR 0005 now
records the one-sidedness of the original blind spot, the residual
[98.995, 99.0) rounding window at precision 2 (three-leg convergence), and
the declined rounding-free fail_under=100 alternative. Doc-only delta;
verification: clean tree 58 tests / 100.00% / exit 0, breach case exits 1.

findings.summary: Docs claimed the blind spot 'closed'; rounding window remains at precision 2
findings.reason: ADR 0005 now states the residual [98.995, 99.0) window explicitly; 'closed' scoped to the specific 0.5-point window PR #6 exposed.
